title: solanum campylacanthum subsp. beniense
intro: Solanum campylacanthum subsp. beniense, also known as Beni solanum, is a plant species in the Solanaceae family, which is native to the Andean region of South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in bioactive compounds, which have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
